observations.recommendation:
1.12. We reiterated the prior year recommendation that the Municipal Accountant:
i. implement COA Circular No. 2023-008 for the one-time cleansing of
dormant accounts; and
ii. continue locating documents and records relative to the undeposited
collections of a previous Treasurer to be able to decide on the validity
of the related receivables.
1.13. We also recommended that the LCE seek assistance from the proper offices in
deciding what actions should be undertaken in the case of the ex-mayor and an
employee.

2.8. We recommended that the:
a) Municipal Treasurer submit the complete list of taxpayers at the
beginning of each year to the Accounting Office as basis in accurately
recording the accounts; and
b) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile their records
to establish correctness of the recorded receivables at yearend.

3.13. We recommended that the:
a) Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer/General Service Officer
maintain Supply Ledger Card and Stock Cards;
b) Inventory Team conduct inventory taking at least twice a year then
prepare the required RPCI; and
c) Accounting Office, Property/Supply Officer and the Inventory
Committee prepare and maintain the forms, registry and reports
prescribed in COA Circular 2022-04 dated May 31, 2022.

4.26. Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that:
a) The Inventory Committee, eventually complete its one-time cleansing to
address the discrepancies in PPE account balances to ensure the accuracy
and reliability of the account reported in the Financial Statements.
b) The Accounting Office indicate to its Notes to the Financial Statements
the required disclosures per COA Circular No. 2020-006.

5.27. We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE instruct the Heads of the
planning and implementing units to –
a) plan intelligently and consider the needs of the constituents in formulating
the developmental plans for the latter to enjoy the benefits that can be
derived from these PPAs; and
b) conduct prioritization of PPAs contained in the AIP so that specific dates
of implementation could be determined for the proper implementation and
monitoring of the same and avoid possible delays during the execution of
projects.

5.28. We further recommended that the:
a) Concerned personnel submit a further justification on the determined
ineligible PPAs funded by the DF; and
b) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat, and other
concerned officials and employees submit necessary documents and/or
explanations for the

6.24. We reiterated that the:
i. concerned officers attach to the DVs, distribution lists completely indicating
necessary details to facilitate the conduct of validation;
ii. concerned officers submit explanation or justification on noted deficiencies in
prior year observation stated in paragraph 7.41 and
iii. LDRRMO review implemented projects and ensure that only allowable
expenses resulting from disaster risk management activities are charged from
LDRRMF.

6.25. We recommended that the concerned personnel prepare the required reports
in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-002 distribution of supplies.

7.20. Based on the above observations, we recommended that:
a) the Municipality conduct assessments using the prescribed GFPS
Functionality Assessment tool to ensure the optimal functionality of its
GFPS and to address any identified gaps in compliance with GAD-related
mandates; and
b) the GFPS eventually adopt the application of the prescribed monitoring
and evaluation tools to determine how much of the Municipality’s actual
disbursements for implementing its major programs can be attributed to
GAD

8.21. Based on the observations regarding SEF compliance and utilization, We
recommended that:
a) the LSB ensure the future distribution of the copy of the SEF Budget to
the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal
Treasurer be promptly accomplished and shall be supported with proper
transmittal records from the concerned departments;
b) the LSB prepare its future SEF budgets with more detail and information
to support transparency and effective evaluation and monitoring;
c) the LSB ensure that all future submissions of quarterly and annual
reports on SEF utilization are supported with proper transmittal records
or proof of receipt and be furnished to the proper concerned agencies
outlined in the Circular; and
d) the Municipality ensure consistent updates across all three postings for
compliance to the Circular

8.22. And in connection with the prior year recommendation, we recommended that the LSB, in coordination with the MTO, is once again advised to coordinate
with DepEd to formalize the agreement requiring personnel to secure a separate
property accountability clearance from the Municipality as a prerequisite for
DepEd office clearance. Any formal agreement, if executed, should be obtained
and made available for verification.

9.9. We recommended and the MTO agreed to examine the books of accounts of more
businesses applying for the renewal of their permits in accordance with the
provisions of LGC and LTOM to properly assess the tax liability of the said
establishments.

10.7. We reiterated our recommendation that Management:
a) revisit the Municipal Ordinances related to the EGR and craft more detailed
guidelines for the operation of the said enterprise;
b) benchmark with private and government memorial parks and gather relevant
data and best practices to be considered in preparing guidelines.

10.8. We further reiterated that disclosures be made on the reported receipts from
cemetery operations that sales of lots were included in the reported receipts.

11.11. However, we reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor
direct the Municipal Accountant to exert best effort to trace and account for the Due
to the PHIC balance.

11.12. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
i) the Municipal Accountant and OIC-HRMO to exert best efforts to
reconcile their records with GSIS and eventually eliminate noted
discrepancies; and
ii) the Municipal Accountant to exhaust all remedies to gather supporting
documents and request for write-off of the balance of Due to PHIC.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the –
a) MTO to –
i) furnish the Municipal Accountant with a list of unreleased checks at the end of the year which will serve as basis in preparing JEV for the restoration of cash and liability accounts; and
ii) see to it that payees fill out the date of receipt column in the Check Registers; and
b) Municipal Accountant to –
i) prepare a JEV restoring cash and liability accounts equivalent to the unreleased checks at the close of the year and another JEV reversing the previous entries made to recognize the availability of the checks for release the following year; and
ii) stop recognizing prepared remittances to BIR and PHIC at year-end as disbursements if there is no actual transfer yet to the said agencies.

We recommended that the accountable officers prepare all the boxes of their RCDs per fund and submit the same, also per fund, for easy review and verification of the said reports.
We also recommended that the Accounting Office forward the RCDs per fund consistent with its Cash Receipt Journals to the Audit Team.

We reiterated our audit recommendations that the LCE –
a) instruct the Accounting Office and MTO to i) prepare and maintain complete PPELCs and PCs, respectively, in accordance with the prescribed guidelines, and
ii) prepare the reconciliation of their records.
b) direct the Accounting Office to –
i) exert more effort to reconcile PPELCs with the GLs, and
ii) compute and recognize allocation of cost/depreciation of the five PPE accounts mentioned in paragraph 3.14; and (
c) require the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit RPCPPE in accordance with the MNGAS for LGUs.

Not Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our audit recommendation that the LCE –
a) instruct the MTO to maintain LRNPCs for all roads under the LGU’s jurisdiction;
b) require the Accounting Office to exert effort in preparing SLs; and
c) direct the Municipal Engineer to assist and/or coordinate in the establishment of correct and verifiable Roadwork Network System.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to –
i) implement COA Circular No. 2023-008 for the one-time cleansing of dormant accounts; and
ii) continue locating documents and records relative to the undeposited collections of a previous Treasurer to be able to decide on the validity of the related receivables.
We also recommended that assistance from proper offices be requested in deciding what actions should be undertaken in the case of the ex-mayor and an employee.
Lastly, we recommended that detailed verification be undertaken for the proper adjustment of the Breeding Stock account.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the LCE instruct the Heads of the planning and implementing units to –
a) plan intelligently and consider the needs of the constituents in formulating the developmental plans for the latter to enjoy the benefits that can be derived from these PPAs; and
b) conduct prioritization of PPAs contained in the AIP so that specific dates of implementation could be determined for the proper implementation and monitoring of the same and avoid possible delays during the execution of projects.
We also recommended that the LCE instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and BAC Secretariat to submit an explanation or justification for the noted deficiencies in paragraphs 6.25 and 6.26.

On the other hand, we reiterated our recommendation that the LCE instruct the LDRMMO and other concerned Heads of Offices to plan judiciously by including PPAs that are responsive to the constituents’ needs.
We further recommend that:
a) concerned Offices –
i) complete the necessary details in the supporting distribution list to facilitate the conduct of validation;
ii) design guidelines on distributing farm inputs to farmers for approval, to prevent excessive disbursements and possible future complaints; and
iii) submit explanation or justification on noted deficiencies in paragraph 7.41; and
b) the LDRRMO review implemented projects and ensure that only allowable expenses resulting from disaster risk management activities are charged from LDRRMF.

Not Implemented

Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that issues presented in paragraph 8.3 be a) explained by the involved Office and b) henceforth avoided.
Likewise, we recommended that relevant guidelines on the PHIC funds be studied for the proper utilization of the said funds.

We recommended that the MTO examine the books of the businesses applying for the renewal of their permits in accordance with the provisions of the LGC and LTOM to properly assess the tax payables of the said establishments.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the concerned Offices to –
a) revisit the Municipal Ordinances related to the EGR and craft a more detailed guidelines in the operation of the said enterprise; and
b) benchmark with private and government memorial parks and gather relevant data and best practices to be considered in preparing guidelines.
We further recommended that disclosure be made on the reported receipts from cemetery operations that sales of lots were included in the reported receipts.

We recommended and the LSB and other concerned Offices agreed to –
a) improve the three-year program so as to have a strategic implementation, and
b) submit the lacking documents and/or explanations on the audit observations presented in paragraph 11.9.
We further recommended that the LSB and the MTO coordinate with the DepEd in order to establish control over properties procured under the SEF.

We recommended that the SWM Board, with MENRO as lead office,
a) harmonize the SWMP with the annual budgets of the Municipality;
b) review the SWMP immediately and identify areas for updating and/or revisions to ensure its sustainability, viability, effectiveness and relevance in relation to local and international developments in the field of solid waste management; and
c) revisit the Ecological SWM Ordinance of the Municipality for its necessary amendments to fully attain the objectives of R.A. No. 9003.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
Not Implemented
Not Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the GFPS ensure that attribution is made in accordance with the HGDG tool and PIMME to determine how much of the Municipality’s actual disbursements for implementing its major programs can be attributed to GAD.
We also recommended that the GFPS constitute its M & E Team to execute a proper monitoring and evaluation system.
We further recommended that –
a) the Municipal Ordinance No. 045, series of 2019, be revisited for a possible updating/revision;
b) detailed guidelines for the dispersal of farm inputs be prepared and officially approved;
c) the GSO/MTO oversee the distribution of rice seeds and fertilizers to the beneficiaries; and
d) if possible, payouts to scholars be undertaken by the Treasury Office.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to exert best effort to trace and account for the Due to PHIC balance.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
i) the Municipal Accountant and OIC-HRMO to exert best efforts to reconcile their records with GSIS and eventually eliminate noted discrepancies; and
ii) the Municipal Accountant to exhaust all remedies to gather supporting documents and request for write-off of the balance of Due to PHIC.

We reiterated our audit recommendation that the Municipal Accountant exhaust all remedies to gather the supporting documents for the receivables noted. (1)

Henceforth, consider the write-off of dormant accounts for more than 10 years, in accordance with the procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2016-005. (2)

 direct the Accounting Office to exert more effort to reconcile PPELCs with the GLs. (4)

Municipal Accountant to establish RPT and SET receivables sourced from the list duly certified by the Treasury Office at the beginning of the year. (7)

The set up of RPT and SET Receivables in CY 2023 was based on the list furnished by the Municipal Treasurer, however, there was no computed tax due for each taxpayer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the complete set of financial statements of the LEEs as prescribed in the Manual so that an accurate basis for managing the operations related thereto will be made. (8)

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality comply with the minimum disaster preparedness standards by 

completing the necessary tools and equipment for disaster readiness and (9)

submitting an explanation to the Audit Team regarding the disparity in the reported number of tools and equipment. (10)

We further recommended that the LCE instruct the Officer-in-Charge (OIC)- LDRMMO and other concerned Heads of the Departments to –

a) plan intelligently by including PPAs that are responsive to the constituents’ needs; and (11)

b) monitor perishable goods like rice, when distributed, to avoid spoilage. (12)

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Heads of the Planning and Implementing Units to –

plan intelligently and consider the needs of the constituents in formulating the developmental plans for the latter to enjoy the benefits that can be derived from these PPAs; (13)

continue to maximize the use of the developmental funds by implementing all the programmed PPAs for the benefit of their constituents; and (14)

conduct prioritization of PPAs contained in the LDIP and AIP so that specific dates of implementation could be determined for the proper implementation and monitoring of the same and avoid possible delays during the execution of projects. (15)

We recommended the LCE to - 

instruct the GFPS to submit the GPB and the GAD AR within the designated deadline of submission to the Audit Team; (16)

coordinate with the concerned departments responsible for the planning and implementation of GAD-related PPAs to submit to the Audit Team GAD reports which are accurate for the latter to perform its mandate properly; (17)

explain the increases in the budget of GAD-related PPAs; (18)

designate a Focal Person for all GAD-related activities to prevent miscommunications between departments as to who will perform such duties and responsibilities needed for the implementation of the GAD-related PPAs; and (19)

submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents for the latter to properly evaluate the GAD PPAs. (20)

We recommended that the LSB –

maximize the use of the SEF budget by implementing all PPAs programmed during the year so that the students can enjoy the benefits expected from the PPAs; and (21)

enjoin the heads of the Property Office of the LGU and DepEd to coordinate with each other to establish accountability for properties procured under SEF. (22)

We recommended that the LCE instruct the –

Officers and employees in charge of implementing projects funded by other agencies to strictly observe the time frame of implementation of the projects to ensure efficient delivery of public service to the intended beneficiaries; (23)

BAC, BAC Secretariat, and the Accounting Office to submit explanations and/or justifications on the other audit observations; and (24)

the Accounting Office to exhaust all remedies to gather the supporting documents for the payables noted. (25)

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE direct the – 

OIC-HRMO to update concerned government agencies on the adjustments in the Municipality’s employ so as to avoid inconsistencies and/or errors in the financial records of the Municipality; (26)

Municipal Accountant to exert best effort to trace and account for the Due to PhilHealth balance and adjust accordingly the year-end balance of the Due to Philhealth account; and (27)

Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer remit immediately all the  premiums and loan amortizations due to appropriate agencies within the prescribed period. (28)

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive make a representation to the Sangguniang Bayan to expedite the revision and updating of the Local                 Revenue Code and adjust and/or update the taxes and fees rates pursuant to Sections 129 and 191 of R.A. No. 7160 to meet the financial needs of the LGU.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this Report

observations.recommendation:
We commended the Management for complying with rules and regulations
applicable to the above-mentioned area and we  recommended  that  Management
continue the same practice in the succeeding reporting period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Bids and
Award Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat, the Municipal Accountant, and the
Municipal Treasurer to submit explanations on the deficiencies presented in
paragraph 2.7.

We recommended that the LCE require the LGU’s Human Resource
Management Officer (HRMO) and other concerned offices to submit the lacking
supporting documents/records/orders to the Audit Team for its evaluation.

We recommended that the LCE enjoin the Municipal Accountant to (i)
exhaust all remedies to gather the supporting documents needed for the receivables
noted; (ii) henceforth, consider the write-off of dormant receivables for more than
10 years in the books of accounts, in accordance with the procedures set forth in
COA Circular No. 2016- 005; and (iii) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to exert
more efforts in establishing the accuracy of the recorded receivables and pursue
collection efforts to recover the funds due to the government.

We reiterated the audit recommendation that the a) Property Officer and the
Accounting Office prepare and maintain complete PCs and PPELCs and reconcile
the same; and b) Inventory Committee tag all PPEs with inventory stickers to ease
the counting of items.

We further recommended that the a) Inventory Team prepare RPCPPE in
accordance with the MNGAS for LGUs; and b) Municipal Mayor instruct the
Accounting Office and the Property Officer to conduct the preliminary activities for
the cleanup of the PPE accounts.

We reiterated the audit recommendation that the Municipal Mayor i)
instruct the concerned Offices to be mindful of their duties laid down in the said
Circular; ii) require the Property Officer to come up with the Local Road Inventory
and Road Map and Local Road Network Property Card for all roads under the
Agency’s jurisdiction; iii) require the Accounting Office to exert effort in preparing
the necessary records and to disclose information on Road Networks in the Notes to
the Financial Statements in accordance with COA Circular 2015-008; and iv)
instruct the Municipal Engineer to assist and/or coordinate in the establishment of
correct and verifiable Roadwork Network System.

We reiterated, for the third time, our audit recommendation that the LCE
make a representation to the SB to expedite the revision and updating of the Local
Revenue Code and adjust and/or update the taxes and fees rates pursuant to
Sections 129 and 191 of R.A. No. 7160 to meet the financial needs of the LGU.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to
maintain special accounts in the GF for the LGU’s LEEs and prepare the complete
set of financial statements thereof as prescribed in the Manual so that an accurate
basis in managing the operations related thereto will be made.

We recommended that the BAC, BAC Secretariat and the Accounting Office
submit the lacking documents and explanation and/or justifications on the other
audit observations.

We recommend that the LCE require the i) BAC, BAC Secretariat, and the
Municipal Accountant to submit explanations on the issues stated in paragraphs
10.9 and 10.10; and ii) OIC-MDRRMO to act on the compliance with the minimum
standards of disaster preparedness.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (i) instruct the concerned officials
and employees on the utilization of TFs to determine and study the accompanying
guidelines of received funds and exert utmost effort to comply with the same; and
(ii) instruct the BAC, the Municipal Accountant, and end user to submit
explanations on the noted issues in paragraph 11.2.

We recommended that a) the LSB (i) formulate the required three-year
program so that utilization of the SEF could be maximized through coordination
with the Municipal and Provincial investment and development plans; and (ii)
submit legal basis on the implementation of one PPA stated in paragraph 12.11 for
the Audit Team’s evaluation; b) the concerned officials and personnel submit the
lacking DVs to the Audit Team; and c) the Property Officer prepare and issue ARE
to the accountable officers for property issued to them.

Furthermore, we reiterated the audit recommendation that the Municipality
coordinate with the DepEd for the purpose of obtaining a mutual agreement with
the DepEd to require DepEd personnel to secure a separate clearance from property
accountability from the LGU as a pre-requisite to the issuance of DepEd office
clearance prior to their retirement, resignation, transfer or re-assignment to be able
69
to recover the physical assets issued to them.

We recommended that the (i) MPDO and Engineering Office continue to
maximize the use of local development funds by implementing all development
projects programmed during the year so that benefits expected from the PPAs can
be enjoyed by the constituents; (ii) heads of planning and implementing units plan
intelligently the local development projects to be implemented, ensuring their
responsiveness to the needs of the constituents; (iii) Municipal Accountant ensure
that all necessary documents to be attached to the DVs are present before making
the payment/s; and (iv) BAC and its Secretariat submit to the Audit Team the
lacking documents as discussed and warrant the completeness of supporting
documents that need to be prepared/submitted to fully display transparent
governance.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:
(a) Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer
to (i) continue their collaborative efforts in pursuing future LGSF-funded
programs in accordance with the guidelines set by the DILG or the DBM, as
the case may be and (ii) coordinate with the DILG officer assigned to the
Municipality for the preparation, implementation and reportorial
requirements that are needed by the DILG in order for the Municipality to
be fully compliant with the requirements set by the DILG;
(b) Municipal Budget Officer and the OIC-LDRRMO to (i) ensure that every
PPA undertaken by the Municipality should be included in the LDRRM Plan
and Annual Investment Plan; and (ii) document any changes and/or any
additions of the PPAs in the LDRRM Plan and Annual Investment Plan and
inform the management immediately hereof should some changes occur ;
(c) BAC and its Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents
as enumerated and warrant the completeness of supporting documents that
need to be prepared/submitted in the procurement stages of projects in
accordance with the provisions of DBM LBC No. 134, DILG MC 2020-088
and R.A. No. 9184 and its Revised IRR; and
(d) Municipal Accountant to ensure that all necessary documents to be attached
to the DVs are present before making the payment/s.

We recommended the GFPS to (i)  plan  activities  which  are  attainable  in
order to maximize GAD funds  for  the  welfare  of  the  constituents;  (ii)  consider
including representatives from the IPs, PWDs, and recognized and/or accredited
NGOs as part of ExeCom’s full compliance with the JMC; (iii) submit the GPB and
the  GAD  AR  within  the  designated  deadline  of  submission  to  the  Audit  Team;  and
(iv.) use HGDG and PIMME tools in assessing and monitoring regular GAD PPAs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) direct the MSWMB to (i)
review every two years or as the need arises, the SWMP for the purpose of ensuring
its sustainability, viability and effectiveness; (ii) prepare an Annual Work Plan and
Budget showing the SWM activities to be implemented and the financial
counterpart for each SWM activity, taking into consideration the initiatives
identified in the 10-year SWM Plan; and (iii) organize monitoring and evaluation
team and require them to prepare Progress Reports in line with the Annual Work
Plan and the SWM Plan; (b) consider reinforcing the MENRO to carve out more
progress in the implementation of SWMP; (c) require the cooperation of the
Barangays in the implementation of Solid Waste Management particularly the
establishment of functional MRF and campaign for proper waste segregation; and
(d) establish a functional MSWMB that is aware of its duties and responsibilities on
ensuring the accomplishment of PPAs included in the SWM Plan.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE direct the (a) Municipal
Accountant to exert best effort to trace and account for the Due to Philhealth and
Due to GSIS balances and accordingly adjust the same so that only accurate
balances are reflected in the books at any given reporting period; (b) OIC-HRMO
to assist and ensure that the Job Order Employees of the LGU secure their own
Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth numbers to avoid late remittances to these government
agencies; and (c) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit
immediately all the due premiums.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the
Sanggunian Bayan, (i) cause the formal constitution of clear guidelines on telephone
expenses by setting the parameters as to eligibility of those who may be qualified to
the benefit, the requirements of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and the reasonable
limit of the monthly entitlement, taking into consideration the value for money
packages offered by telecommunication companies vis-à-vis the actual need to carry
out official tasks; (ii) ensure the inclusion in the policy guidelines of a provision
guaranteeing Municipality’s ownership over the mobile phone units in the postpaid
plans; and (iii) direct the concerned officials to submit the required certification of
official calls to support a claim for reimbursement/payment of communication
expense.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor continue to instruct the
Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to i) monitor and cause the
immediate settlement of the balances of audit suspensions by persons responsible
either through cash payments or submission of the lacking information or
documents within the prescribed period; and ii) require persons liable named in the
audit disallowances to settle their accounts through cash payments, unless they are
97
awaiting COA decisions on their appeal.

We recommended that the:

the concerned Municipal officials and personnel submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team and observe compliance with the above-cited provisions of GPPB  Circular in the emergency procurement of goods; and

the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to (i) adhere to the fundamentals of recording purchases of materials, equipment and medicines; and (ii) coordinate with the concerned offices to immediately submit the DVs amounting to ₱150,675.38.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officials to refrain from charging against the LDRRMF expenses which are not in keeping with the purposes declared by the above-cited law.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive:

enjoin the concerned Municipal official/s to confer with other implementors of emergency subsidy program with the end view of collecting the ₱50,000.00 excess assistance distributed to ten beneficiaries who received multiple assistance from other government agencies; and

instruct the MSWD Officer to: (i) coordinate with DSWD Regional Office No. III and request the latter to rectify the list uploaded in its website such that the name of one individual who returned the SAP 1st Tranche subsidy should be dropped from the list; and ii) justify why the two JO personnel were considered beneficiaries of SAP subsidy despite a prohibition to the contrary and immediately demand for the restitution of the amount erroneously given to said two JO personnel.

We reiterated our last year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (a) direct the Inventory Committee of the Municipality to conduct a complete physical count of all PPE and label all properties/equipment showing complete description for easy identification and monitoring; and (b) require the Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to maintain complete and updated ledger cards and property cards, respectively, and periodically conduct reconciliation thereon.

We likewise recommended that the Local Chief Executive avail the one-time cleansing of  PPE account balances established under COA Circular No. 2020-006 so that the verifiability thereof as to existence, condition and accountability would be realized.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Planning Development Office (MPDO) to stop the inclusion of PPA/s or expenditure/s which are not in accordance with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and ensure that the unimplemented PPAs are fully delivered in the second year of implementation.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) direct the Inventory Committee of the Municipality to (i) conduct a complete physical count of all PPE and label all properties/equipment showing complete description for easy identification and monitoring; and (ii) submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year; and (b) require the Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to maintain complete and updated ledger cards and property cards, respectively, and periodically conduct reconciliation thereon
observations.reason: Verification made by the Audit Team disclosed that there were still attending issues that need to be addressed, thus, reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the (a) Management agreed to exert best effort in following up with the DILG Regional Office No. III the status of the request for approval of the revised POW so that the agency can start implementing the pre-identified AM-funded projects; (b) Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO), agreed to observe the timeline of project completion and strictly monitor or supervise the implementation of project activities to avoid the incurrence of delays; and (c)  Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to the above-cited provision of R.A. No. 9184 and its RIRR in the procurement activities.

We recommended and the (a) Management agreed to stop the inclusion of PPAs or expenditures which are not in accordance with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1; (b) Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the BAC, Technical Working Group and BAC Secretariat to submit the lacking documentary requirements to the Audit Team; (c) Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) and other concerned offices agreed to ensure that the appropriate PPAs which were not implemented in CY 2019 are fully delivered in the second year of implementation in order to deliver the needed socio-economic and environmental developments; (d) Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the MPDO, agreed to observe the timeline of project completion and strictly monitor or supervise the implementation of project activities to avoid the incurrence of delays and to attain early the desired objectives of the projects; and (e) Management agreed to observe the rule in accounting for obligations by taking up in the registries amounts committed to be paid by the agency once the related contracts between the Municipality and contractor were perfected.
observations.reason:
(a) Reiterated in this report

(c) Verification and inspection made by the Audit Team revealed that continuing projects to be carried out in CY 2020 were partially implemented. As per Management, citing issue with project sites easily engulfed with water as reason for non-implementation of some of the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive, together with the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, agreed to (a) carefully plan and implement programs, projects and activities under the LDRRM to attain utmost results and deliver the desired services to the residents of the Municipality; and (b) strictly conform with the provisions of the cited COA Circular on the preparation of prescribed LDRRMFIP and inclusion therein of the activities funded not only from the current appropriation but also those from unexpended balances from previous years.

We recommended and the Management agreed to (a) update the 10-year solid waste management plan accordingly, observe its timely revision and submission and endeavour to secure the immediate approval of the plan; (b) provide logistical support through integration in AIP of the implementation of SWM programs/segments determined in the 10-year solid waste management plan, so as to obtain the intended purpose of the said law and to ensure the unceasing local government’s commitment and participation towards achieving a clean and healthy environment; and (c) come up with a concrete plan relative to the utilization of existing central MRF.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the EXECOM and TWG to (a) comply with the provision of the JMC with respect to constitution of GFPS in accordance with Item 4.1.A.2 of the said law; (b) consider utilizing all available resources to update the GAD database; (c) submit, in writing, the changes in the DILG-approved GPB and secure an acknowledgement receipt of adjusted GPB from the DILG and fully integrate thereof in the annual budget and corresponding AIP of the Municipality; (d) monitor the GAD PPAs and coordinate with concerned offices the implementation of GAD Plan and preparation of GAD AR; (e) submit copy of Annual GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report to the COA Audit Team within the prescribed deadline; and (f) establish a GAD M&E Team to enhance, strengthen and maintain gender responsive monitoring and evaluation system on the outcomes of the LGU GAD policies, programs and projects.

While we took cognizance of the relentless efforts of the LGU in supporting public basic education, the Audit Team recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) (a) in coordination with the members of the LSB, agreed to require the latter to follow and perform the activities in the preparation and submission of the SEF Utilization Report with indicative timelines to DepEd CO and post the same in at least three (3) conspicuous public places for transparency and accountability as prescribed by the abovementioned circulars; and (b) agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers and documentary attachments.

We recommended and the Management agreed to (a) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and submission of financial reports so that opportune verification and post-audit activities will be facilitated; and (b) keenly observe the deadline of submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team.
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to continue their rigid abidance to the aforementioned revenue regulation and laws and prompt remittance of the taxes due to the BIR.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive should: (a) direct the Inventory Committee of the Municipality to (i) conduct a complete physical count of PPE, investigate the cause of missing items, take immediate and appropriate action so that the persons accountable therefor be held personally liable for their loss; and (ii) label all properties/equipment showing complete description for easy identification and monitoring; and (b) require the designated Property Custodian and the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete and updated property cards and ledger cards, respectively, and periodically conduct reconciliation thereon..

(b) provide logistical support through integration in AIP of the implementation of SWM programs/segments determined in the 10-year solid waste management plan, so as to obtain the intended purpose of the said law and to ensure the unceasing local government’s commitment and participation towards achieving a clean and healthy environment; and

(a) in coordination with the members of the LSB, agreed to require the latter to follow and perform the activities in the preparation and submission of the SEF Utilization Report with indicative timelines to DepEd CO and post the same in at least three (3) conspicuous public places for transparency and accountability as prescribed by the abovementioned circulars; and
(b) agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers and documentary attachments.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the:
(a) Accounting Office to (i) exert best effort to gather pertinent documents or records to establish, trace and account for the unremitted combined amount of ₱81,507.21 due to Pag-IBIG and Philhealth; and (ii) adjust accordingly the year-end balance for the account Due to GSIS; and
(b) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit all the due premiums, when evidence so warrants.
We recommended and the Management agreed to continue to exert efforts to enforce settlement of all disallowances as required under COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that management should (a) strictly conform with the provisions of the abovementioned regulations in preparing the necessary LDRRMFIP which include activities to be funded from LDRRM current appropriation and from unexpended balance from previous years and (b) ensure its compliance with respect to minimum standards on disaster preparedness.
observations.reason: Verification made by the Audit Team revealed that the Municipality prepared LDRRMFIP but overlooked to consider the previous’ years unexpended balance in their current year plan. Moreover, positions for LDRRM Officer II was already occupied, however, the Management still remained incompliant to acquire the equipage needed for disaster preparedness.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee and its Secretariat, Accounting Office and the Engineering Office submit the lacking documents and strictly adhere to the above-cited provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR and COA regulations respecting submission of documentary requirements.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local Chief Executive instruct (a) the Municipal Treasurer to exert more efforts in establishing the accuracy of the recorded receivables and pursue collection efforts to recover the funds due the government; and (b) the Municipal Accountant to undertake the necessary procedures in dropping some of these dormant accounts from the books through adhering to the guidelines and procedures set by COA Circular No. 2016-005.

8.6	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to (a) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to delay the post-audit activities and communication of the results thereof; and (b) observe the prescribed period on the submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team.

9.5	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to strictly comply with all the provisions of the aforementioned COA Circular in order to enhance transparency on its operations and monitoring of its PPAs.
11.5	We recommended that the Municipality continuously adhere to R.A. No. 8291, R.A. No. 9679 and the National Health Insurance Act of 2013.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to exert more efforts in establishing the accuracy of the recorded receivables and pursue collection efforts to recover the funds due the government; and the Municipal Accountant to undertake the necessary procedures in dropping some of these dormant accounts from the books through adhering to the guidelines and procedures set by COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: To issue demand letters and to request write-off for doubtful accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:  

   direct the Inventory Committee of the Municipality:
1)  to conduct a complete physical count of PPE, giving priority to locate the missing properties and if established missing, take immediate and appropriate action so that the persons accountable therefor be held personally liable for their loss; and
2) submit the required inventory report on time for the audit team’s assessment and validation;  

   require the designated Property Custodian and the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete and updated property cards and ledger cards, respectively, for reconciliation and control purposes; and

   instruct the Property/Supply Officer to label all properties/ equipment, showing complete information/description for easy identification and monitoring.
observations.managementAction:
Physical Inventory will be conducted before year ends.
Property and ledger cards were prepared for every PPE purchased.
Some PPE’s were already labelled with property/sticker tag.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor spearhead to a) stop the practice of paying various expenses through reimbursement basis; and b) strictly adhere to the requirement of rules and regulations that all payments must be made by check directly to the suppliers.

We commended the efforts exerted by the Management, headed by the Local Chief Executive, in the utilization of the 20% Development Fund and recommend to continue maximizing the utilization of their funds to provide continuous benefits to its constituents.
We recommended that the Municipality through its Local Chief Executive strictly adhere to the prescribed rules and regulations of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01.

While we took cognizance of the relentless efforts of the LGU to support gender equality and women empowerment, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):

Sign appropriate issuances to institutionalize and strengthen the GAD Focal Point System;

Spearhead, together with the other members of the GAD Inter-Agency Committee, in facilitating the establishment of the GAD Database to aid in gender analysis and provide baseline data on the status of women and men in the LGU; and

Direct the GAD Focal Person to assess the extent of compliance through a compliance checklist of the elements of the institutional mechanisms in place and intensify efforts on those areas that should be in place.

We reiterated our previous audit recommendation that the Local School Board coordinate with the Property Offices of both the LGU and the DepEd to strengthen the controls on property accountability to monitor the clearances of those of the DepEd personnel who are retiring, resigning or terminating their employment.

We recommended that a) management ensure that claims against government funds be supported with the required documents provided under the abovementioned regulations; and (b) the Municipal Accountant make sure that the transactions are supported with complete documentation before certifying and effecting payment in order to avoid suspension and /or disallowance of the transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) coordinate the efforts of public and private sectors and implement the five basic environmental laws, especially the establishment of a Materials Recovery Facility, on preserving and protecting the environment and public health as required under R.A. No. 9003. We further recommended that the LCE encourage the full participation of the barangays in the implementation of the law.
observations.managementAction:
Presently, a lot is identified & being assessed as to suitability for the establishment of MRF.
Prioritize purchase of lot.
Wide coverage of IEC be conducted to households and students. Provision of garbage containers in public places & posting of posters/billboards/tarpaulins on proper waste segregation & disposal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly comply with the requirements on the submission to COA of contracts/ purchase orders and supporting documents within five days from its perfection/approval. | CY 2016 AAR

1.6	We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to exert more efforts in establishing the accuracy of the recorded receivables and pursue collection efforts to recover the funds due the government; and the Municipal Accountant to undertake the necessary procedures in dropping some of these dormant accounts from the books through adhering to the guidelines and procedures set by COA Circular No. 2016-005.

2.8      We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
direct the Inventory Committee of the Municipality:
1)  to conduct a complete physical count of PPE, giving priority to locate the missing properties and if established missing, take immediate and appropriate action so that the persons accountable therefor be held personally liable for their loss; and
2) submit the required inventory report on time for the audit team’s assessment and validation;
require the designated Property Custodian and the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete and updated property cards and ledger cards, respectively, for reconciliation and control purposes; and
instruct the Property/Supply Officer to label all properties/ equipment, showing complete information/description for easy identification and monitoring.

3.4 	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor spearhead to a) stop the practice of paying various expenses through reimbursement basis; and b) strictly adhere to the requirement of rules and regulations that all payments must be made by check directly to the suppliers.

4.3	We commended the efforts exerted by the Management, headed by the Local Chief Executive, in the utilization of the 20% Development Fund and recommend to continue maximizing the utilization of their funds to provide continuous benefits to its constituents.
5.5		We recommended that the Municipality through its Local Chief Executive strictly adhere to the prescribed rules and regulations of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01.

6.6	While we took cognizance of the relentless efforts of the LGU to support gender equality and women empowerment, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
Sign appropriate issuances to institutionalize and strengthen the GAD Focal
Point System;
Spearhead, together with the other members of the GAD Inter-Agency   Committee, in facilitating the establishment of the GAD Database to aid in gender analysis and provide baseline data on the status of women and men in the LGU; and
Direct the GAD Focal Person to assess the extent of compliance through a compliance checklist of the elements of the institutional mechanisms in place and intensify efforts on those areas that should be in place.

7.5		We reiterated our previous audit recommendation that the Local School Board coordinate with the Property Offices of both the LGU and the DepEd to strengthen the controls on property accountability to monitor the clearances of those of the DepEd personnel who are retiring, resigning or terminating their employment.

8.4 	We recommended that a) management ensure that claims against government funds be supported with the required documents provided under the abovementioned regulations; and (b) the Municipal Accountant make sure that the transactions are supported with complete documentation before certifying and effecting payment in order to avoid suspension and /or disallowance of the transactions.

9.15		We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) coordinate the efforts of public and private sectors and implement the five basic environmental laws, especially the establishment of a Materials Recovery Facility, on preserving and protecting the environment and public health as required under R.A. No. 9003. We further recommended that the LCE encourage the full participation of the barangays in the implementation of the law.

10.6	We recommended that Management strictly comply with the requirements on the submission to COA of contracts/ purchase orders and supporting documents within five days from its perfection/approval.
12.2 	We recommended that the Municipality continuously adhere to R.A. No. 8291.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer exert effort in establishing the accuracy of the recorded receivables and pursue collection efforts to recover the funds due the government; and the Municipal Accountant undertake the necessary procedures in dropping some of these dormant accounts from the books as provided under COA Resolution Nos. 80-122 and 2003-002 dated April 23, 1980 and January 30, 2003, respectively.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive stop the practice of granting cash advances to persons who are not permanent officials of the Municipality to avoid risk of non-liquidation thereof and possible loss of government funds.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to either return the unutilized balance of P8,849.25 to the source agency or deposit the amount to the National Treasury.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the Inventory Committee to give priority in locating the missing properties. If established missing, take immediate and appropriate action so that the persons accountable and responsible therefor be held personally liable for their loss; and make necessary adjustments in the accounting and property records. We further recommended that the extent of the conduct of physical inventory be monitored and followed-up to ensure that the physical inventory report will be prepared and submitted on time.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive effect immediately the insurance of its properties in accordance with the Administrative Order and COA Circular to indemnify the government in case of loss through fire or theft and other calamities.

Moreover, the Acting Property/ Supply Officer should furnish the GSIS an updated property inventory report that are not yet covered by insurance for the appraisal of the amount of premium to be paid.
observations.reason: The amount is exorbitant and given the financial status of the Municipality, it cannot disburse such funds needed for insurance coverage.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Disposal Committee, which was created by the agency, follow the COA Revised Guidelines on Appraisal of Property and prepare an Agency Appraisal Report to facilitate proper evaluation and disposal of these unserviceable properties and waste materials the soonest possible time. Also, we recommended that all unserviceable properties and waste materials be properly recorded and accounted for.

We recommended that management, headed by the Local Chief Executive,:

revisit and follow the provisions of the Municipal Revenue Code that require:

all business establishments with at least gross receipts over P500,000.00 per annum to support with documents, such as audited financial statements and BIR Business Tax Return (VAT & Percentage Tax Remittance), the sworn statements of gross sales receipts for the preceding calendar year;

the Treasurer’s Office to prepare a stratified schedule of presumptive income level to approximate the gross receipt of each business classification; and

(b) require the Municipal Treasurer to inspect business establishments within the jurisdiction of the Municipality, as mandated under Section 470 (d)(4) of the Local Government Code, to ensure effective and efficient collection of business taxes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the minimum limit of P3,000 as employees’ monthly net take home pay pursuant to Section 48 of GAA 2015. We likewise recommended that statutory deductions be given preference over other deductions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the implementing office and personnel concerned to see to it that projects covered by fund transfers are implemented immediately from receipt thereof so as not to delay the enjoyment by the intended beneficiaries of the benefits that may be derived therefrom.

We recommended that the provisions for warranty security under the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 be strictly complied with to ensure guarantee for indemnity in case the goods delivered are found to be of substandard quality or defective.

We recommended that management, headed by the Local Chief Executive, maximize the utilization of the 20% development fund for its constituents to enjoy the benefits of the planned programs, projects and activities in a timely manner.

We recommended that the Local School Board:

 prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute;

request for inputs from the DepEd official/ School Division Superintendent in the prioritization of SEF projects and expenditures in accordance with the DepEd criteria and with due regard to the provisions of laws, rules and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund; and

coordinate with the Property Offices of both the LGU and the DepEd to strengthen the controls on property accountability to monitor the clearances of those of the DepEd personnel who are retiring, resigning or terminating their employment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the (a) Accountant to disclose the details of the unexpended balance of the fund in the Notes to Financial Statements to provide adequate information on the Fund; and (b) the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to refrain from using DRRMF for programs/ projects/ activities which are not directly related to disaster preparedness, mitigation, response and rescue operations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the GAD Focal Point to comply with laws, rules and regulations pertaining to GAD and appropriate at least five percent (5%) of the Agency’s total budget to ensure sufficient resources for the implementation of the required planned programs and projects and activities to address gender and development issues.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) coordinate the efforts of public and private sectors and implement the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment and public health as required under R.A. No. 9003. We further recommended that the LCE encourage the full participation of the barangays in the implementation of the law.
We recommended that Management continue to hire personnel based on necessity and payment of claims for services rendered by casual and job order personnel be adequately supported with documents.
We recommended the continuous maintenance of subsidiary ledger for allocations to the Municipality for proper monitoring thereof. | CY 2015 AAR
